topic: Autofocusing an input on page load
competing_claims: Autofocusing the primary field (e.g. a search box or first form field) speeds up task start and signals where to begin.
Autofocus on load can disorient screen-reader and keyboard users, cause unexpected scroll jumps, and trigger the on-screen keyboard prematurely on mobile.
resolution: Autofocus is appropriate on single-purpose pages whose sole task is that input (a dedicated search page, a login page, a one-field step) and where it will not move the viewport unexpectedly. Avoid autofocus on content-rich pages, on mobile where it forces the keyboard up over content, and anywhere it would skip past meaningful context for assistive-technology users. When used, ensure focus placement is announced and does not cause a disorienting scroll.
unresolved: Acceptable autofocus behaviour on mixed content/form pages for assistive-technology users remains debated.
